name: Tenable Vulnerability Management
description: Cloud vulnerability management platform (formerly Tenable.io) built on Nessus scanning. Yields scan result and coverage evidence, findings reports and dashboard exports, scheduled report deliveries, scan policy configuration and full vulnerability/asset datasets via the REST API.

steps.action: Log in to Tenable Vulnerability Management (cloud.tenable.com) and open the Scans page; screenshot the scan list showing scan names, targets, schedules and last-run status as scan-coverage evidence
steps.permissions_required: Scan Operator or higher (Basic users see only scans shared with them)
steps.security_note: Use a named individual or dedicated evidence-collection account, not a shared login

steps.action: Open a completed scan for the collection period and export the results (Nessus, CSV or HTML format) via the scan's Export option
steps.permissions_required: Scan Operator or higher with Can View on the scan
steps.audit_note: Filtered exports are not supported for scan results older than 60 days, and unfiltered .nessus exports truncate plugin output at 5 MB — export promptly after each scan window

steps.action: Go to Explore > Findings, apply your severity and asset filters, and generate a Findings report (PDF) or CSV export capturing the current vulnerability position
steps.permissions_required: Standard or higher with Can View on the assets
steps.audit_note: The generated report is timestamped by Tenable and reflects the filter set — record the filters used so successive periods are comparable

steps.action: Screenshot the scan configuration evidence: open Scans > User Defined templates and capture the scan policy settings (credentialed checks, plugin families, assessment settings) for the in-scope scans
steps.permissions_required: Template owner, or user with Can Use/Can Edit template permissions
steps.audit_note: Policy configuration evidences how scans assess targets (e.g. authenticated scanning enabled), which auditors weigh alongside the results themselves

steps.action: Upload the exports and screenshots to the evidence repository with the evidence ID reference
steps.security_note: Scan exports enumerate exploitable weaknesses per asset — restrict access to authorised personnel and check before sharing outside the security team

L2.title: Tenable VM Scheduled Scans and Reports

steps.action: Confirm each in-scope scan has a recurring schedule: in the scan's Basic Settings, enable Schedule and set the frequency, start time and timezone so results are generated on a defined cadence
steps.permissions_required: Scan Manager or Administrator (or scan owner with configure rights)
steps.audit_note: Scheduled scans paired with scheduled reports evidence continuous operation of the vulnerability management process

steps.action: Click Reports in the left navigation and create or select a report template covering the in-scope assets and severities (My Report Templates or All Report Templates tab)
steps.permissions_required: Scan Operator, Standard, Scan Manager or Administrator

steps.action: Select the template's check box and click Schedule; set the recurrence and email recipients, and enable the password protection toggle so the emailed PDF requires a password to open
steps.permissions_required: Report owner, Administrator, or a user granted Can Configure on the report
steps.security_note: Share the report password through a separate channel from the report emails
steps.audit_note: Each scheduled run produces a vendor-timestamped report artefact on a fixed cadence

steps.action: Deliver the scheduled reports to a restricted, monitored compliance mailbox and file each report into the evidence repository on receipt
steps.security_note: Limit mailbox access to authorised personnel; report contents identify vulnerable assets

steps.action: Set a recurring calendar check to catch missed report emails or failed scheduled scans, and record any gaps with the reason
steps.audit_note: Unexplained gaps in a scheduled evidence series weaken the audit trail

L3.title: Tenable VM REST API Automated Collection

steps.action: Create a dedicated least-privilege service user (e.g. Basic role with Can View permissions on in-scope assets, or a custom role with the VM.VM_EXPLORE.VM_EXPLORE.EXPORT privilege), then generate its API keys via My Account > API Keys > Generate
steps.permissions_required: Administrator (create user and enable API access)
steps.security_note: The access and secret keys are shown only once and regenerating replaces any existing keys — store them immediately in a secrets manager and rotate on a defined schedule

steps.action: Authenticate API calls with the header 'X-ApiKeys: accessKey=<ACCESS_KEY>;secretKey=<SECRET_KEY>' against https://cloud.tenable.com
steps.security_note: Read the keys from the secrets manager at runtime and ensure pipeline logs never print the X-ApiKeys header

steps.action: Export vulnerability data: POST https://cloud.tenable.com/vulns/export with filters (severity, state, since for incremental runs), poll GET /vulns/export/{export_uuid}/status until FINISHED, then download each chunk via GET /vulns/export/{export_uuid}/chunks/{chunk_id}
steps.permissions_required: Basic role or VM.VM_EXPLORE.VM_EXPLORE.EXPORT privilege, plus Can View on the exported assets
steps.audit_note: Include the fixed state in filters so remediation closure is captured, not just open findings; chunks expire 24 hours after creation, so download within the same run

steps.action: Export the asset inventory with POST https://cloud.tenable.com/assets/export (same status/chunk polling pattern) and reconcile it against your CMDB to evidence that scanning covers all in-scope assets
steps.permissions_required: Service account with Can View on the assets
steps.audit_note: Coverage reconciliation evidences completeness of scanning scope, not merely that scans run

steps.action: Archive raw scan results on a cadence: POST https://cloud.tenable.com/scans/{scan_id}/export (format nessus or csv), poll the export status, then download the file for the evidence store
steps.permissions_required: Scan Operator or higher with Can View on the scan
steps.audit_note: The .nessus file preserves full per-plugin output and scan policy metadata, the strongest artefact for auditor re-verification

steps.action: Schedule the collection script daily via cron or a CI/CD pipeline with retry on HTTP 429 (honouring the Retry-After header), alerting on failed runs, and validation that chunk counts and finding totals are consistent with the previous run's baseline
steps.audit_note: Run-over-run validation provides assurance of collection completeness

L4.title: Tenable VM Managed Evidence Pipeline with Metrics

steps.action: Tenable Vulnerability Management has no native outbound webhooks or event streaming — the closest real capability is frequent incremental exports: run POST /vulns/export hourly with since set to the last successful run and state filters covering open, reopened and fixed
steps.audit_note: Advance the since watermark only after a verified successful download so no findings are skipped between runs

steps.action: Deploy the Tenable Add-on for Splunk (TA-tenable) to continuously pull vulnerability and asset data into your SIEM, giving indexed, long-term retention independent of Tenable's export windows
steps.permissions_required: Administrator (Tenable API keys for the add-on); Splunk administrator
steps.security_note: Configure the add-on with the dedicated service account's keys and store them in Splunk's encrypted credential store
steps.audit_note: SIEM retention provides tamper-evident storage and removes reliance on 24-hour chunk availability

steps.action: Collect the platform activity log continuously via GET https://cloud.tenable.com/audit-log/v1/events (filterable by date) to evidence administrative changes to scans, policies and users
steps.audit_note: Tenable retains activity log data for 3 years; regular collection into your own store preserves it beyond that and supports change-control evidence

steps.action: Reconcile daily: compare the asset export against expected inventory and flag assets whose last scan time exceeds the scan-frequency SLA, computing a coverage percentage as the pipeline's core completeness metric
steps.audit_note: Asset-versus-inventory reconciliation evidences that every in-scope asset is being assessed on schedule

steps.action: Run automated quality checks and a collection-health dashboard: export failures, gaps in the incremental time series, SLA breaches, remediation ageing trends, with alerts on any gap or threshold breach
steps.audit_note: The dashboard serves as continuous evidence of a managed collection process

steps.action: Generate an automated quarterly collection-health report summarising scan coverage, export gaps, SLA breaches and remediation, and file it in the evidence repository
